title: The Art of Negotiation
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Successful (0) negotiation requires a combination of skill, patience and preparation. Good negotiators demonstrate (17) confidence during discussions and are able to communicate their position with (18) clarity. They also show great (19) flexibility, adapting their approach to meet the needs of all parties involved.||Listening (20) carefully is just as important as speaking, as it allows negotiators to understand what the other side truly wants. Finding (21) creative solutions that benefit everyone leads to more (22) durable agreements. A lack of (23) honesty can quickly undermine trust and make it harder to reach any (24) satisfactory outcome for all those involved.

example.text: Successful (0) NEGOTIATION requires a combination of skill, patience and preparation. (NEGOTIATE)

questions.explanation: The noun 'confidence' is formed from 'confident' by replacing '-ent' with '-ence'.
The noun 'clarity' is formed from 'clear' by adding the suffix '-ity' and adjusting the spelling.
The noun 'flexibility' is formed from 'flexible' by changing '-ble' to '-bility'.
The adverb 'carefully' is formed from 'careful' by adding the suffix '-ly'.
The adjective 'creative' is formed from 'create' by adding the suffix '-ive'.
The adjective 'durable' shares the root 'dur-' with 'duration'; it is formed by adding '-able'.
The noun 'honesty' is formed from 'honest' by adding the suffix '-y'.
The adjective 'satisfactory' is formed from 'satisfy' by changing '-y' to '-actory'.
